positions.reasoning: Highest score of the group at 75 with a clear bullish thesis in quantum computing — a sector with genuine long-term tailwinds. Price is near the middle of the starter zone, giving us room to add lower if the tape stays weak.
Score of 71 and bullish on voice AI, a real near-term commercial theme. Small 2% starter keeps risk tight — this is a higher-volatility name that can move hard in both directions in a bear tape.

Score 70, bullish, and RKLB has real revenue and launch cadence behind it — not pure speculation. Near the top of the starter zone so sizing stays conservative at 3%, with intent to add on any flush toward $60.

passed.reason: Score of 69 with a Cautious tag is a yellow flag in this environment. AI drug discovery is a long-duration thesis and this stock has shown it can cut in half fast — passing until the tape stabilizes or a cleaner entry appears.

Lowest score of the bullish names at 65 and delivery robotics is still early-stage with real execution risk. In a bear tape I want higher conviction before opening; watching for a pullback toward $7 to reassess.

Score 57 and Cautious — weakest setup in the group. Price is right at the top of the starter zone at $22.02, meaning there's no cushion. Digital health faces regulatory and competitive headwinds; not worth the risk here.

grahamNote: Opening three starter positions for a combined 8% deployment — deliberately light given VIX near 27 and a clear bear tape. IONQ, RKLB, and SOUN represent the three highest-conviction names with real sector catalysts. The remaining 92% stays in cash, ready to average down on these three or open new positions if the market finds a floor. Measure twice, cut once — no sense rushing it.
